message.content:
Run CODEX (GPT-5.6) to gate the checkpoint wiring. Write /tmp/ckpt_gate.md then 'cd /tmp && timeout 240 codex exec --skip-git-repo-check - < /tmp/ckpt_gate.md 2>&1 | tail -30' (embed evidence inline; medium effort; no sandbox flags; bubblewrap warning fine).
Context: wired the generation checkpoint. wire={"files_changed":["/home/polaris/wt/phase2c/src/polaris_graph/honest_pipeline.py"],"save_wiring":"Save added in run_honest_pipeline immediately BEFORE the strict_verify verdict at src/polaris_graph/honest_pipeline.py (the call `strict = strict_verify(draft_text, evidence_pool, quantified_models=...)`, originally line 344). Right after `evidence_pool = {ev["evidence_id"]: ev for ev in evidence}` is built, a flag-gated block runs: `if _pg2c_checkpoint_enabled(): _pg2c_save_precheck(run_id, draft_text, evidence_pool)`. _pg2c_save_precheck writes ONLY {run_id, draft_text, evidence_pool} as JSON to PG_CHECKPOINT_DIR/pg2c_precheck_{run_id}.json — never the strict_verify verdict. Verified at runtime: saved file keys are exactly [draft_text, evidence_pool, run_id], no strict/verdict/faithfulness field. Pipeline B (graph.py) was already fully wired (get_checkpointer at 1428, compile checkpointer at 1501-1502, LangGraph autosaves full pre-check ResearchState per node, verdict nodes re-run on resume) — left untouched.","reload_wiring":"Reload added at the TOP of run_honest_pipeline (src/polaris_graph/honest_pipeline.py), right after `run_dir_path` is created and before the Phase 2b scope gate. Flag-gated: `if _pg2c_checkpoint_enabled(): _pg2c_reloaded = _pg2c_load_precheck(run_id); if _pg2c_reloaded is not None: draft_text = _pg2c_reloaded.get("draft_text", draft_text)`. _pg2c_load_precheck returns only {draft_text, evidence_pool, run_id} (never a verdict). Execution then falls through normally to the Phase 4 step where strict_verify(draft_text, evidence_pool, ...) runs FROM SCRATCH on the reloaded draft — the verdict is recomputed, never restored from disk. Three helper functions were added near the top of the module (after the logger): _pg2c_checkpoint_enabled(), _pg2c_checkpoint_path(run_id), _pg2c_save_precheck(...), _pg2c_load_precheck(...). checkpoint_manager's AsyncSqliteSaver could not be reused here because pipeline A is a synchronous non-LangGraph orchestrator with no ResearchState/graph; the localized JSON helpers reuse the same resolve() accessor and PG_CHECKPOINT_DIR for consistency.","flag_off_is_noop":true,"collection_after":"16738 tests collected, 11 errors in 11.61s — matches the expected 16738/11 baseline exactly. The 11 collection errors are pre-existing and unrelated to this change. Flag-OFF is byte-identical: both the save and reload call sites are wrapped entirely in `if _pg2c_checkpoint_enabled():`, and _pg2c_checkpoint_enabled() returns `resolve("PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED") == "1"` with default '0' (config_defaults.py:97, default unchanged). Verified at runtime: flag default -> False, flag=1 -> True, flag=0 -> False. When the flag is off no checkpoint file is written, no new branch is taken, and draft_text is unmodified — no changed ordering. py_compile passed on the changed file."}. validate={"flag_off_oracle_sha":"9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98","flag_off_byte_identical":true,"collection_ok":true,"characterization_ok":true,"roundtrip_test_passes":true,"no_verdict_persisted":true,"notes":"All four gates green on worktree /home/polaris/wt/phase2c (branch chore/review-readiness-phase2c, HEAD c5448ec).

GATE 1 — FLAG-OFF byte-identical (critical): PASS. Oracle replay SHA = 9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98 (matches required golden exactly). "replay artifact BYTE-IDENTICAL to recorded golden" + "ACCEPTANCE PASSED: all run controls valid" (exit 0). Verified BOTH with PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED unset AND explicitly =0 — identical SHA both ways. Proves normal runs are unchanged when the flag is off.

GATE 2 — Collection unchanged: PASS. "16738 tests collected, 11 errors" — matches the expected 16738/11 baseline exactly. Oracle overlay cassettes present (acceptance_golden.json, acceptance_llm.jsonl, acceptance_retrieval.jsonl, demo_artifact.jsonl, demo_llm.jsonl).

GATE 3 — Config characterization: PASS. tests/test_config_registry.py + tests/test_settings_models.py = 9 passed, 0 failed.

GATE 4 — FLAG-ON roundtrip (NEW test): PASS, 4/4. Wrote /home/polaris/wt/phase2c/tests/test_checkpoint_roundtrip.py exercising the real Plan V4 2C generation-checkpoint wiring in src/polaris_graph/honest_pipeline.py (_pg2c_save_precheck / _pg2c_load_precheck), hermetic (no network; PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED=1 and PG_CHECKPOINT_DIR set via monkeypatch to tmp_path; strict_verify stubbed with a sentinel).
  (a) checkpoint artifact IS written (pg2c_precheck_<run_id>.json) and contains the drafts/outline — the draft_text (with '# Outline'/'## Section' headers) plus the evidence_pool. PASS.
  (b) artifact contains NO faithfulness/strict_verify verdict field: persisted keys are EXACTLY {run_id, draft_text, evidence_pool}; asserted absence of faithfulness/faithfulness_score/faithfulness_pct/strict/strict_verify/verdict/kept_sentences/dropped_sentences/verification/verified/passed both structurally and as raw-byte substrings. PASS.
  (c) reload re-runs verification (does not read a verdict from disk): _pg2c_load_precheck returns pre-check data only (verdict-free); a sentinel-patched strict_verify is proven to be invoked exactly once on the reloaded draft — mirroring honest_pipeline.py Phase 4 (line 453) where strict_verify runs from scratch on the restored draft after reload (line 303-306). PASS.

NO VERDICT PERSISTED — confirmed. The 2C checkpoint is deliberately pre-check-only: honest_pipeline.py saves {run_id, draft_text, evidence_pool} BEFORE strict_verify is computed (line 445-453) and on reload restores only draft_text (line 303-306), then recomputes strict_verify at line 453. A poisoned/partial verdict can never be trusted from disk. The design comment at lines 70-80 and 297-302 documents this invariant, and the test locks it in.

Note on scope: honest_pipeline.py is a synchronous non-LangGraph orchestrator; its 2C checkpoint (JSON, pre-check only) is separate from the heavier LangGraph AsyncSqliteSaver in src/polaris_graph/checkpoint_manager.py (A2 traceback/rewind). The task's roundtrip contract (drafts/outline saved, no verdict, reload re-verifies) maps precisely to the 2C JSON path, which is what the new test targets."}. Requirements: flag-off byte-identical (oracle SHA 9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98 unchanged), save PRE-CHECK data only (no faithfulness verdict persisted), reload re-verifies from scratch.
Ask codex: "Is the flag-off path provably byte-identical (write fully guarded by PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED, default unchanged, oracle SHA unchanged)? Does the checkpoint persist ONLY pre-check data (drafts/outline) and NEVER a faithfulness/strict_verify verdict, so a resumed run cannot trust a stale verdict? Does the reload path re-run verification from scratch? Any way a poisoned/partial checkpoint could corrupt a resumed run's faithfulness? End with: CHECKPOINT-SAFE or CHECKPOINT-REVISE."
Return verdict + points.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: `draft_text` is a required parameter, so the reload at line 306 (`_pg2c_reloaded.get("draft_text", draft_text)`) always has a valid fallback. Config default is `'0'`. Everything checks out. Now let me write the gate prompt with inline evidence.

input.content:
You are a senior code reviewer gating a generation-checkpoint wiring change for faithfulness safety. Answer only from the evidence embedded below. Medium effort. End your reply with exactly one token on its own line: CHECKPOINT-SAFE or CHECKPOINT-REVISE.

## What was wired

A generation checkpoint was added to Pipeline A (`src/polaris_graph/honest_pipeline.py`), a SYNCHRONOUS non-LangGraph orchestrator. It saves PRE-CHECK draft data before the faithfulness verdict is computed, and on restart reloads only the draft and re-verifies from scratch. Pipeline B (LangGraph, graph.py) was already wired and left untouched.

## Hard requirements to gate

1. FLAG-OFF byte-identical: every read AND write fully guarded by `PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED`, default unchanged ('0'), oracle replay SHA unchanged `9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98`.
2. Save PRE-CHECK data ONLY — never a faithfulness / strict_verify verdict persisted.
3. Reload re-runs verification from scratch — the verdict is recomputed, never restored from disk.
4. A poisoned/partial checkpoint must not be able to corrupt a resumed run's faithfulness.

## EVIDENCE (verbatim from worktree /home/polaris/wt/phase2c, branch chore/review-readiness-phase2c, HEAD c5448ec)

### Config default (src/polaris_graph/config_defaults.py)
```
96:    'PG_CHECKPOINT_DIR': 'state',
97:    'PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED': '0',
```

### Helper functions + design invariant (honest_pipeline.py lines 76-157)
```python
# 76 poisoned/partial verdict can never be trusted from disk.
# 78 SAFETY: every read/write below is gated behind PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED (default
# 79 '0'). When the flag is OFF, _pg2c_checkpoint_enabled() returns False and none
# 80 of this code runs — the generation path is byte-identical to today.

def _pg2c_checkpoint_enabled() -> bool:
    """True only when PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED == '1'. Default is '0' (off)."""
    from src.polaris_graph.settings import resolve
    return resolve("PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED") == "1"

def _pg2c_checkpoint_path(run_id: str) -> Path:
    """Location of the pre-check draft checkpoint for a run (never a verdict)."""
    from src.polaris_graph.settings import resolve
    return Path(resolve("PG_CHECKPOINT_DIR")) / f"pg2c_precheck_{run_id}.json"

def _pg2c_save_precheck(run_id: str, draft_text: str, evidence_pool: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    """Persist pre-check data (draft + evidence) so a restart can re-verify.
    Writes ONLY the draft and the retrieved evidence_pool — never the
    strict_verify outcome. Best-effort: any failure is logged and swallowed."""
    try:
        path = _pg2c_checkpoint_path(run_id)
        path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        path.write_text(
            json.dumps(
                {"run_id": run_id, "draft_text": draft_text, "evidence_pool": evidence_pool},
                indent=2, sort_keys=True, default=str,
            ) + "\n",
            encoding="utf-8",
        )
        logger.info("[polaris graph] 2C: saved pre-check checkpoint for %s (%s)", run_id, path)
    except Exception as exc:  # pragma: no cover - best effort
        logger.warning("[polaris graph] 2C: failed to save pre-check checkpoint for %s: %s", run_id, str(exc)[:200])

def _pg2c_load_precheck(run_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
    """Load pre-check data (draft + evidence_pool) if a checkpoint exists.
    Returns None when no usable checkpoint is present. NEVER returns a verdict —
    the caller re-runs strict_verify from scratch on the loaded draft."""
    try:
        path = _pg2c_checkpoint_path(run_id)
        if not path.exists():
            return None
        data = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
        if not isinstance(data, dict) or "draft_text" not in data:
            return None
        logger.info("[polaris graph] 2C: reloaded pre-check checkpoint for %s — "
                    "re-running strict_verify from scratch (no saved verdict trusted)", run_id)
        return data
    except Exception as exc:  # pragma: no cover - best effort
        logger.warning("[polaris graph] 2C: failed to load pre-check checkpoint for %s: %s", run_id, str(exc)[:200])
        return None
```

```python
    run_dir_path = Path(run_dir)
    run_dir_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    # Plan V4 2C: reload pre-check checkpoint (flag-gated). On restart with
    # PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED=1 and a checkpoint present, restore the saved draft

        if _pg2c_reloaded is not None:
            draft_text = _pg2c_reloaded.get("draft_text", draft_text)

```

### SAVE call site + strict_verify (Phase 4, lines 444-456)
```python
    evidence_pool = {ev["evidence_id"]: ev for ev in evidence}
    # Plan V4 2C: save pre-check checkpoint (flag-gated). Persist draft +
    # evidence_pool as pre-check resume data BEFORE the strict_verify verdict is
    # computed — the verdict itself is never saved, so a resumed run always
    # re-verifies from scratch. The entire write is guarded by
    # PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED; with the flag OFF this block is a no-op.
    if _pg2c_checkpoint_enabled():
        _pg2c_save_precheck(run_id, draft_text, evidence_pool)
    strict = strict_verify(draft_text, evidence_pool, quantified_models=quantified_models)
    rendered_text, biblio = resolve_provenance_to_citations(strict.kept_sentences, evidence_pool)
```

## Validation gates already run (worktree phase2c, HEAD c5448ec)
- GATE 1 FLAG-OFF byte-identical: PASS. Oracle replay SHA = 9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98 (matches golden). Verified BOTH with PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED unset AND =0 — identical SHA both ways.
- GATE 2 Collection: 16738 tests collected, 11 pre-existing errors — matches baseline exactly.
- GATE 3 Config characterization: test_config_registry + test_settings_models = 9 passed.
- GATE 4 FLAG-ON roundtrip (new hermetic test, 4/4): (a) artifact pg2c_precheck_<run_id>.json IS written with draft_text + evidence_pool; (b) persisted keys are EXACTLY {run_id, draft_text, evidence_pool} — asserted absence of faithfulness/faithfulness_score/strict/strict_verify/verdict/kept_sentences/dropped_sentences/verified/passed both structurally and as raw-byte substrings; (c) sentinel-patched strict_verify proven invoked exactly once on the reloaded draft.

## Questions to answer
1. Is the flag-off path provably byte-identical (write AND read fully guarded by `_pg2c_checkpoint_enabled()`, default '0' unchanged, oracle SHA unchanged)?
2. Does the checkpoint persist ONLY pre-check data (draft/evidence_pool) and NEVER a faithfulness/strict_verify verdict, so a resumed run cannot trust a stale verdict?
3. Does the reload path re-run verification from scratch (strict_verify recomputed on the reloaded draft, not restored)?
4. Any way a poisoned/partial checkpoint could corrupt a resumed run's faithfulness? (Consider: malformed JSON, missing draft_text key, a hostile draft_text swapped on disk, evidence_pool tampering, best-effort swallow of errors.)

End with exactly one line: CHECKPOINT-SAFE or CHECKPOINT-REVISE.

I independently verified the evidence against the actual worktree source before running the gate: config default `'0'` (config_defaults.py:97), both call sites guarded by `_pg2c_checkpoint_enabled()`, save persists only `{run_id, draft_text, evidence_pool}` before strict_verify, reload restores only draft_text with a valid parameter fallback.

codex_points: CODEX (GPT-5.6, medium effort) returned CHECKPOINT-SAFE, exit 0. Evidence independently verified against worktree /home/polaris/wt/phase2c HEAD c5448ec before gating.

Q1 Flag-off byte-identical: YES. Both the read (line 303) and write (line 451) call sites are fully guarded by _pg2c_checkpoint_enabled(); config default PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED='0' unchanged (config_defaults.py:97); oracle SHA 9c0a3d43...db98 reproduced with the flag both unset and explicitly =0.

Q2 Pre-check data only: YES. _pg2c_save_precheck writes exactly {run_id, draft_text, evidence_pool} and runs BEFORE strict_verify (save at 451-452, strict_verify at 453), so the faithfulness verdict physically cannot be persisted. GATE 4 test asserts absence of every verdict-shaped key both structurally and as raw-byte substrings.

Q3 Reload re-verifies from scratch: YES. _pg2c_load_precheck restores only draft_text (line 306); strict_verify is then invoked fresh on that draft at line 453; the sentinel test confirms exactly one fresh invocation. No verdict is read from disk.

Q4 Poisoned/partial checkpoint cannot corrupt faithfulness: confirmed. Malformed JSON, missing draft_text key, and load errors all fall back to the supplied draft_text (a REQUIRED kwarg, so the .get() default is always valid); save errors are swallowed and never skip verification; stored evidence_pool is never restored so tampering is inert. A hostile draft swapped on disk can replace candidate content but must still pass fresh strict_verify and renders only from strict.kept_sentences — no verdict bypass.

Non-blocking robustness note (CODEX): _pg2c_load_precheck does not type-validate that draft_text is a str or that run_id matches; this is an availability/robustness concern only, not a path to restoring or bypassing a verdict. Does not block CHECKPOINT-SAFE.
Scope note: gate covers Pipeline A (honest_pipeline.py) synchronous JSON pre-check checkpoint only; Pipeline B LangGraph AsyncSqliteSaver was left untouched.
